node.statement: N1.2 (spectral idempotents intertwine: Ftilde j = j P). Apply GT-intertwine to the pair F (acting on D, i.e. on the unital Banach algebra End-side via the UCP lift) and Phi (on B(H)_sa), with intertwiner j. The intertwining hypothesis Fj=jPhi holds: F=jC and Phi=Cj give Fj=jCj=j(Cj)=jPhi. The threshold hypotheses hold: ||F^2-F||<=||F^2-F||_cb<=eta<1/4 (GT-hyp) and ||Phi^2-Phi||<=eta<1/4 (N1.1). j is bounded (||j||_op=1 by GT-hyp order-isometry) and unital (j(I)=I, GT-hyp). Hence GT-intertwine gives theta(2F-1) j = j theta(2Phi-1), i.e. Ftilde j = j P (Ftilde=theta(2F-1), P=theta(2Phi-1)).
